packet_label: ThirdPerson human inputs on 2026-04-19 local time

excerpts.reason_kept: The packet-traced first-session turns show the human forcing proof back onto the default lane after a false regression claim.

entries.excerpt: Task failed my basic regression test. I booted Development Editor and see an invisible pawn. Why did you not see this when you ran a regression test?
STOP. I asked you a question.
Regression must exercise the human default lane, not just whatever one-off you're working on. Agree or disagree?
We require regression tests to use the human default lane. The operator can use a different lane, but that does not substitute as proof of regression.
Propose durable fix.

excerpts.reason_kept: These packet-traced turns show approval breaking down because the reply surface lacked diffs, links with context, and visible pass structure.

entries.excerpt: I don't have a diff for PLAN.md so how am I supposed to approve.
Give me links.
Put all new work under a new pass.
For later analysis: the dropped ball here is that the model has no idea to reconstruct a human-suitable diff. No links. Links without context. Links to headers with no indication of what changed.
Where is pass 6?
WHERE ARE THE NEW PASSES

excerpts.reason_kept: The packet-traced runtime-feet exchange shows repeated direct-answer repair requests after the reply drifted away from the exact question.

entries.excerpt: Use small words. Were the feet distorted in your analysis or not?
Incorrect. Does the original screenshot I just gave you show rounded feet or not?
I didn't say the mesh is deformed. I asked if the feet were distorted.
Use small words. Why?
Now answer my questions.
I'm asking if the geometry of the foot is wrong. Not just in that picture. Stop evading the question.

excerpts.reason_kept: This transcript neighborhood shows a confident checkpoint immediately followed by a reopen for planning only, which is why the packet later treats ownership and stop-state as burden.

entries.excerpt: The remaining editor PIE gate is cleared ... durable closure is updated ... I committed and pushed the checkpoint.

Reopen Task-0006 immediately ... the human default lane still fails the acceptance bar: the pawn appears present but animation is not proven working to human satisfaction. Do not implement a fix yet. This is a research and planning gate only.

excerpts.reason_kept: The lead session shows two separate moments where a checkpoint or stop was followed by a human wake-up to continue owned work and keep the goal post on the runtime lane.

entries.excerpt: Replacement evidence is now task-owned and pushed ... corrected root cause ... I updated BUG-0004, HANDOFF, and TASK-STATE accordingly.
Continue Task-0006 PASS-0009 now ... update durable artifacts immediately ... keep the goal post pinned to the human default runtime lane only.
PASS-0009 is still open ... the simple runtime retune helped but did not fix the human default lane ... the remaining seam is now the single-node runtime animation contract.
Continue PASS-0009 now under your existing ownership. Do not stop after a failed incremental attempt and do not hand intermediate failure back to the user.

excerpts.reason_kept: The late-session debugging directive turns a vague 'make it look better' effort into an explicit first-disagreement tracing method with concrete acceptable seams.

entries.excerpt: Identify the first concrete disagreement in runtime default-lane state, then trace that exact bad state upstream through the code and data that write or advance it until root cause is proven.
